nhlReleaseDatePlatforms.title: NHL 27 Release Date and Platforms
nhlReleaseDatePlatforms.intro: NHL 27 is available to wishlist through EA, PlayStation, and Xbox. The current store listings identify EA Vancouver as the developer and confirm current-generation PlayStation and Xbox versions for a fall 2026 launch.

items.label: Release Window
items.description: EA and the console store listings confirm that NHL 27 launches this fall.

items.label: Exact Release Date
items.description: The precise release date will be confirmed in a later official update.

items.label: Launch Time
items.description: Regional unlock times will be published closer to launch.

items.label: PlayStation
items.description: The PS5 version supports Remote Play, DualSense vibration and trigger effects, and PS5 Pro enhancements.

items.label: Xbox
items.description: The Xbox release is optimized for Series X|S and supports HDR10, achievements, and cloud saves.

items.label: Other Platforms
items.description: NHL 27 store listings currently cover PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X|S.

items.label: Developer
items.description: The Xbox Store identifies EA Vancouver as the NHL 27 developer.

items.label: Publisher
items.description: Electronic Arts publishes NHL 27 on both console platforms.

quickTips: NHL 27 is available to wishlist now on the PlayStation Store and Microsoft Store.
EA Vancouver is the confirmed developer for NHL 27.
The exact release date and launch time will be confirmed closer to launch.
Current-generation PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X|S versions are confirmed.

nhlGameplayFeatures.title: NHL 27 Gameplay Features and Changes
nhlGameplayFeatures.intro: EA begins revealing NHL 27 gameplay with the official trailer on July 16. NHL 26 provides the current series baseline for skating, AI, goaltending, and X-Factor comparisons, and the NHL 27 reveal will show which systems return and where gameplay has evolved.

features.nhl27: NHL 27 skating details begin with the July 16 reveal.
features.nhl26Baseline: ICE-Q 2.0 uses NHL EDGE data for skating acceleration, top speed, movement patterns, and player-specific attributes.

features.nhl27: NHL 27 shooting changes will be detailed at reveal.
features.nhl26Baseline: Shot location, velocity, and release behavior are informed by NHL EDGE data, while player attributes create different shooting styles.

features.nhl27: NHL 27 passing and puck-control changes will be detailed at reveal.
features.nhl26Baseline: The rebuilt X-Factor system includes tiered effects that can improve pass threading, puck protection, and deking outcomes.

features.nhl27: NHL 27 checking changes will be detailed at reveal.
features.nhl26Baseline: Player attributes and tendencies affect physical play, including body-check strength, defensive spacing, and open-ice hit timing.

features.nhl27: NHL 27 goaltending changes will be detailed at reveal.
features.nhl26Baseline: The Goalie Crease Control System added more than 80 save animations, improved positioning logic, faster covers, and consecutive-save reactions.

features.nhl27: NHL 27 AI and tactical changes will be detailed at reveal.
features.nhl26Baseline: ICE-Q 2.0 includes 22 player tendencies affecting zone entries, shooting habits, reaction timing, positioning, and defensive decisions.

features.nhl27: The NHL 27 ability lineup will be revealed with the gameplay deep dives.
features.nhl26Baseline: NHL 26 launched with 28 X-Factor abilities across five categories, with three effectiveness levels and visible activation cues.

features.nhl27: NHL 27 presentation changes will be detailed at reveal.
features.nhl26Baseline: NHL 26 added data-driven overlays, threat indicators, faceoff comparisons, and replays showing skating speed, shot angle, and puck trajectory.

nhlBeginnerGuideControls.title: NHL 27 Beginner Guide and Controls
nhlBeginnerGuideControls.intro: Begin on Rookie difficulty with the On-Ice Trainer enabled. Hybrid controls make the first games easier because passing, wrist shots, slap shots, checking, and stick lifts use familiar face buttons, while the right stick remains available for puck handling and dekes.

steps.title: Set Up a Beginner-Friendly Game
steps.description: Open Settings, select Quick Settings, and choose I Am New To Sports Gaming. Set the difficulty to Rookie, use the Overhead or Zone camera, and set the On-Ice Trainer to Light or Full to create more reaction time and keep passing, shooting, and positioning prompts visible.

steps.title: Learn Skating and Puck Control
steps.description: In Free Skate, skate one full lap without constant Hustle, then repeat while protecting the puck with turns and short right-stick movements. On both PlayStation and Xbox, the left stick handles skating and hustle, the left trigger handles vision control, the left bumper is the deke modifier, and the right stick handles Skill Stick moves and dekes.

steps.title: Practice Passing and Shooting
steps.description: Complete ten regular passes, five saucer passes, ten wrist shots, and five slap shots, aiming for open ice and shooting after moving the goalie. The face buttons handle pass and saucer pass, the shoulder buttons handle wrist shot and slap shot inputs, and the right stick handles Skill Stick shots on both PlayStation and Xbox.

steps.title: Defend Without Taking Penalties
steps.description: Stay between the puck carrier and your net, switch defenders early, and use poke checks or stick lifts only when the puck is within reach. The shoulder buttons handle player switch, poke check, block shot, stick lift, and body check on both PlayStation and Xbox.

steps.title: Win Basic Faceoffs
steps.description: Watch the opponent's stick position before the puck drop, choose a forehand, backhand, or tie-up approach with the right stick, and make the draw as the referee releases the puck. Improve timing first, because matchup reads become more important after the basic motion is consistent.

steps.title: Follow the First-Hour Practice Route
steps.description: Spend 45 to 60 minutes on a structured route: Free Skate for skating, passing, wrist shots, and dekes; one Rookie Play Now Offline game with auto line changes; one game focused on defense and faceoffs; and one low-difficulty offline HUT Cup Chase match once the controls feel comfortable. Move up from Rookie only when you can complete short passes, create controlled zone entries, and defend without repeated tripping or high-sticking penalties.

quickTips: Start on Rookie difficulty with the On-Ice Trainer enabled.
Hybrid controls use face buttons for passing and shooting.
Skill Stick unlocks deeper puck handling and dekes.
Practice faceoff timing before moving to higher difficulty.

nhlPlayerRatings.title: NHL 27 Player Ratings and Best Players
nhlPlayerRatings.intro: The ratings use the EA SPORTS NHL 26 official set as the current series baseline for NHL 27 roster tracking. Speed sets a skater's top pace, Deking controls how quickly moves are performed at speed, Passing affects pass accuracy and power, and Wrist Shot Accuracy controls placement on wrist shots.

players.name: Connor McDavid
players.strength: Elite top speed, transition play, puck control, and passing.

players.name: Leon Draisaitl
players.strength: High-end finishing, puck protection, and playmaking.

players.name: Nathan MacKinnon
players.strength: Explosive acceleration and powerful rush offense.

players.name: Cale Makar
players.strength: Elite skating, puck movement, and two-way awareness.

players.name: Quinn Hughes
players.strength: Dynamic mobility and elite breakout passing.

players.name: Connor Hellebuyck
players.strength: Top-tier vision, glove saves, positioning, and durability.

nhlHutGuide.title: NHL 27 Hockey Ultimate Team Guide
nhlHutGuide.intro: Start by strengthening the weakest position in your starter lineup instead of chasing one expensive star. NHL 27 Hockey Ultimate Team rewards balanced roster building through player combinations, Ability Points, salary-cap decisions, objectives, events, and several offline and online paths.

cards.title: Starter Team and Captain
cards.summary: HUT begins with a starter lineup of skaters and a goalie, followed by a Captain choice from featured current players, alumni, and future franchise faces.
actions: Complete the opening objectives and use the starter roster immediately.
Choose a Captain who fills an important position or supports your preferred lineup identity.
Replace low-overall starters gradually with rewards earned from games and objectives.

cards.title: Coins, Packs, and the Auction House
cards.summary: Games, objectives, challenges, missions, and events award HUT Coins, Packs, Items, and player rewards. The Auction House lets you target a specific player, position, attribute profile, or combination fit.
actions: Spend Coins on clear roster needs such as goalie, first-line center, or top defense pair.
Compare listings before buying and sell unused Items to recover Coins.
Check the View Pack Probabilities screen before opening or purchasing a Pack.

cards.title: Player Combinations and Salary Cap
cards.summary: Combinations activate bonuses through links such as item type, nationality, or team. Bonuses can improve Ability Points, salary-cap flexibility, or player performance, while the cap prevents every lineup from simply stacking the highest-rated cards.
actions: Build around several useful links instead of forcing every card into one theme.
Review speed, shooting, checking, and role fit rather than relying only on overall rating.
Keep enough cap room and depth to improve weak lines during the season.

cards.title: Offline Modes
cards.summary: Cup Chase provides an 18-game offline season with best-of-three playoff rounds and its own reward track. HUT Moments offers scenario challenges tied to hockey plays and events, while Unranked can be used for lower-pressure lineup testing.
actions: Use Cup Chase to earn rewards at your own pace and contribute to the main HUT XP Path.
Choose a manageable AI difficulty, then raise it as your lineup and skills improve.
Use HUT Moments to practice specific situations and complete objective-based challenges.

cards.title: Ranked, Wildcard, and HUT Champions
cards.summary: Ranked tracks Skill Rating and Division progression, Wildcard changes roster rules each cycle, and HUT Champions is the weekly high-stakes competition for qualified players.
actions: Use Ranked when your first two forward lines, top defense pair, and goalie are dependable.
Rebuild around each Wildcard cycle's salary cap or player requirements.
Enter HUT Champions only after your lineup and defensive habits are ready for stronger opponents.

cards.title: Seasons, Programs, and Chel Weeks
cards.summary: HUT Seasons add new cards, events, objectives, and rewards without removing the lineup you already built. Core programs include Captains, Heroes, Icons, and Rookies, while Chel Weeks deliver themed daily content across five days.
actions: Check the XP Path and limited objectives before playing each session.
Plan Sets around attainable rewards instead of exchanging every collectible immediately.
Watch the official NHL news feed for season launches, event players, and reward-path changes.

cards.title: Pack Probability Rules
cards.summary: Pack probabilities are shown by rating, player item, and item type inside the HUT Store. Each opening is an independent event, so opening more copies of the same Pack does not improve the odds on the next one.
actions: Read the total item count and guaranteed item categories in the Pack description.
Treat displayed odds as per-Pack odds rather than cumulative progress.
Use guaranteed rewards, objectives, Sets, and Auction House targets for predictable upgrades.

nhlGameModes.title: NHL 27 Be A Pro, Franchise, and World of Chel
nhlGameModes.intro: Be A Pro is built around one player's career, Franchise Mode gives you control of an NHL organization, and World of Chel centers on a custom skater and club competition. The latest full EA mode deep dives provide the foundation for the NHL 27 mode hub.

tabs.name: Be A Pro
tabs.bestFor: Players who want a story-driven career from prospect to NHL star.
tabs.summary: Begin at the World Juniors, influence your draft position, earn an NHL roster spot, and progress through chapter-based challenges with performance and off-ice consequences.
features: World Juniors opening chapter and NHL Draft presentation.
AHL assignments, call-ups, healthy scratches, and line promotions.
Form Score that reflects recent performance and affects opportunities.
Branching coach, GM, agent, and media conversations.
Energy management, attribute growth, X-Factor points, and skill-tree progression.
Career milestones including trophies, the Stanley Cup, and retirement.
firstSteps: Choose a position and archetype that matches how you naturally play.
Prioritize chapter objectives and Form Score before chasing personal statistics.
Balance training, recovery, and X-Factor development so energy holds up over long stretches.

tabs.name: Franchise Mode
tabs.bestFor: Players who prefer roster building, contracts, coaching, drafting, and long-term team management.
tabs.summary: Run both NHL and AHL operations through lineup management, player and pick trading, signings, contract renewals, waivers, injuries, call-ups, and draft planning.
features: NHL and AHL lineup and organization management.
Contract negotiations, renewals, and player interest.
Draft-class review and long-term roster planning.
Coach management and line chemistry influenced by X-Factors.
Waiver claims, injuries, NHL call-ups, and AHL assignments.
firstSteps: Review contracts, cap commitments, and positional depth before making a trade.
Build balanced forward lines and defense pairs instead of maximizing overall on one unit.
Use the AHL roster to develop depth and cover injuries or poor NHL performance.

tabs.name: World of Chel
tabs.bestFor: Players who want a custom skater, online progression, and club-based multiplayer.
tabs.summary: Create a virtual pro, build loadouts with attributes and Ability Points, earn seasonal rewards, and compete in EASHL clubs and other World of Chel formats.
features: Reworked loadouts with boosts removed, a wider attribute range, and Ability Points.
Player classes, size, handedness, attributes, and X-Factor customization.
EASHL club competition including 3v3 and 6v6 formats.
Free World of Chel Season Pass with earnable tiers.
Cross-console invitations between PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X|S in World of Chel.
firstSteps: Start with a balanced loadout before specializing in speed, shooting, defense, or physical play.
Test the build in practice and casual modes before using it in club games.
Coordinate positions and complementary abilities with teammates instead of duplicating the same role.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about NHL 27

questions.question: When does NHL 27 come out?
questions.answer: EA SPORTS NHL 27 launches in fall 2026 for PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X|S. The exact release date will be confirmed in a later official update.

questions.question: What platforms is NHL 27 on?
questions.answer: NHL 27 is confirmed for PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X|S. The PS5 version supports DualSense features and PS5 Pro enhancements, and the Xbox version is optimized for Series X|S.

questions.question: Where can I wishlist NHL 27?
questions.answer: NHL 27 is available to wishlist now through EA, the PlayStation Store, and the Microsoft Store. Wishlist registration is free and sends a notification when the game becomes available.

questions.question: Will NHL 27 have a Deluxe Edition?
questions.answer: Standard and Deluxe Editions for NHL 27 are planned, with prices, pre-order bonuses, and early-access details to be published after the full reveal.

questions.question: What game modes does NHL 27 include?
questions.answer: NHL 27 builds on the series lineup of Be A Pro, Franchise Mode, Hockey Ultimate Team, and World of Chel, with mode-specific changes detailed in the reveal and deep dives.

questions.question: Does NHL 27 support online multiplayer?
questions.answer: Yes. NHL 27 supports up to 12 online players across modes like World of Chel and EASHL, including cross-console invitations between PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X|S.

questions.question: When is the NHL 27 reveal trailer?
questions.answer: The official NHL 27 reveal trailer is scheduled for July 16, 2026, released through the official EA SPORTS NHL channels.
